channel.title: MockForge — Engineering Notes
channel.description: Engineering notes and release deep dives from MockForge.

item.title: MockForge vs Postman Mock Server
item.description: An honest comparison of Postman Mock Server and MockForge — when each tool is the right choice, the gaps you'll hit as your architecture grows, and the pricing tradeoffs at scale.

item.title: Conformance Testing Maturity: Multi-Protocol Spec Validation and SARIF Reports
item.description: How MockForge's conformance testing framework grew from basic spec validation into a structured 47-check framework with SARIF output, per-category targeting, and multi-protocol contract checking.

item.title: Release Notes: gRPC Dynamic Routing, $ref Resolution, and Protocol Handler Refactor
item.description: How the v0.3.70–v0.3.75 cycle consolidated gRPC dynamic routing, $ref resolution for OpenAPI specs, and interior-mutable protocol handlers that enable live config updates.

item.title: Extending MockForge with WASM Plugins
item.description: Custom mocks without forking the codebase — write response generators, data transformers, and protocol handlers in any WASM-compilable language, sandboxed and simple to wire up.

item.title: Fault Injection and Chaos Patterns Across Protocols
item.description: Static mocks give false confidence — inject controlled failures across HTTP, gRPC, Kafka, and WebSocket to surface the error-handling gaps your integration tests miss.

item.title: Our $10/mo SaaS Infrastructure
item.description: A walkthrough of MockForge Cloud's hosting stack — Cloudflare Pages for the admin UI, Fly.io for compute, Neon for Postgres — and why it costs under $10/month at launch traffic.

item.title: How We Built the Hosted-Mock Cost Cap
item.description: A pre-launch readiness review surfaced a 10x cost-leak risk on the $29 Pro plan. The retrospective on how we shipped body-size caps and per-deployment RPS limits in the week before going live.

item.title: When WireMock Isn't Enough
item.description: WireMock has been the default HTTP mock server for fifteen years. The specific cases where it stops being the right tool — and the ones where switching would be a step backward.

item.title: Mocking Kafka Producers Without a Real Broker
item.description: Three levels of fake, when each is the right choice, and how a wire-compatible Kafka mock fits between client-library stubs and full testcontainers — including deterministic failure-injection patterns you can't reproduce on a real broker.

item.title: Why MockForge Stayed Multi-Protocol
item.description: The market is full of HTTP-only mock services. Why we took the harder path: protocols beyond HTTP, spec-driven mocking that generalizes, and what the trade actually costs.

item.title: How to Mock a gRPC Service in CI
item.description: A practical guide: from the proto file to a gRPC mock in CI, with reflection probing and a GitHub Actions snippet. No JVM, no testcontainers, no hand-rolled fake to maintain.

item.title: MockForge Cloud is Here
item.description: After two years building the OSS multi-protocol mock server, the managed version is live. Deploy a hosted mock from a spec in 30 seconds — and keep the OSS path you already had.

item.title: Multi-Target Testing, Real-World Spec Support, and Conformance Maturity
item.description: Two weeks of field testing against 66+ proxy targets and real-world specs from GitHub, Amazon S3, and Xero drove multi-target conformance, HAR-to-YAML generation, field-level schema errors, and Swagger 2.0 formData support.

item.title: API Drift Detection and Contract Alerting
item.description: Why we treat contract drift as an alerting event and how endpoint-level mismatch metadata shortens triage loops.

item.title: v0.3 Migration Playbook: Reduce Upgrade Risk in Three Passes
item.description: A practical migration sequence for moving into v0.3 safely: baseline drift, stage strictness, then move critical routes with rollback toggles.

item.title: Spec-Driven OpenAPI Conformance Testing in MockForge
item.description: How the 0.3.55 to 0.3.57 releases moved MockForge from generic API checks into spec-driven conformance with response schema validation and SARIF output.

item.title: Spec-Driven Conformance: Why Contract Verification Beats Generic Checks
item.description: Spec-driven OpenAPI conformance turns API testing from generic request checks into real contract verification against the features your spec actually uses.

item.title: How We Took WAFBench SQLi Detection from 37/46 to 46/46
item.description: A technical deep dive into the 0.3.46 to 0.3.54 security-testing hardening arc across payload delivery, cookie handling, grouped CRS requests, and measured SQLi detection gains.

item.title: WAFBench Security Hardening: Transport Semantics and Benchmark Credibility
item.description: Security benchmarks are only trustworthy when payload delivery is right. What it takes to move from partial coverage to meaningful WAFBench results.

item.title: Recent MockForge Reliability Updates: Admin Startup, Better Failure Reporting, and Correct Response-Code Checks
item.description: A short product update on the 0.3.72 and 0.3.73 releases covering admin startup reliability, clearer failed-check reporting, and more accurate conformance behavior.

item.title: Reliability Update: Admin Startup, Failure Reporting, and Conformance Accuracy
item.description: Small reliability releases matter when they remove first-run friction and make failed checks easier to trust. What the 0.3.72 and 0.3.73 releases improved.

item.title: Release Engineering Note: Security Guardrails from v0.2.x
item.description: A focused review of v0.2.x hardening work: validation defaults, auth-related flow safety, and migration priorities for teams upgrading from earlier versions.

item.title: How We Use Scenario Switching to Keep Mocks Honest
item.description: Scenario switching helps teams test stateful behavior instead of static happy-path fixtures. This note outlines a practical scenario model and why it improves integration confidence.

item.title: Release Cadence and Risk: How We Keep Fast Iteration Safe
item.description: How we sequence fixes, hardening, and communication so release speed does not become release risk.

item.title: Client Generation Tradeoffs: Safety, Speed, and Developer Experience
item.description: A practical note on balancing strict schema fidelity with developer velocity in generated clients.

item.title: v0.2.7: API Sync, TCP Support & Advanced Mocking
item.description: Intelligent API synchronization with change detection, TCP protocol mocking, flexible response selection modes, and real webhook execution.

item.title: Why Failure-Path Testing Should Be a First-Class Mocking Requirement
item.description: Controlled failure profiles expose retry and fallback weaknesses before integration surprises hit teams.

item.title: v0.2.6: Production Security & Infrastructure
item.description: Enterprise-grade TLS/HTTPS and mTLS support, built-in tunneling for local development, and completed SDK implementation phases 1 and 2.

item.title: v0.2.5: Enterprise Security & OAuth2
item.description: Complete OAuth2 with PKCE, structured error handling, request/response validation, interceptors, and multiple authentication methods for production-ready API clients.

item.title: v0.2.4: Client Generator Fixes & Test Coverage
item.description: Request body generation fixed across React, Vue, and Svelte. TypeScript required vs optional fields corrected. Comprehensive multi-framework test coverage added.

item.title: v0.2.3: Bug Fixes & Stability Improvements
item.description: Critical fixes for OpenAPI example extraction, request body parameter generation, and React client generator Handlebars template logic.

item.title: v0.2.0: Multi-Framework Client Generation
item.description: Generate type-safe API clients for React, Vue, Angular, and Svelte from OpenAPI specs. Unified spec parser, enhanced mock data generation, and browser mobile proxy mode.

item.title: Introducing MockForge: First Public Release (v0.1.2)
item.description: The first public release of MockForge: AI-powered API mocking for REST, gRPC, GraphQL, WebSockets, and SMTP in a unified Rust framework.

item.title: v0.1.3: Protocol Expansion & Testing Tools
item.description: Native GraphQL handlers, programmable WebSockets, HTTP scenario switching, capture scrubbing, unified config profiles, and the mockforge-test integration crate.

item.title: Migration Guide: Moving from Static Fixtures to Stateful Mocks
item.description: A staged migration path from static fixtures to state-aware scenario switching for better integration confidence.
